notes: status.json is authoritative for hub state.
.plans carries planning, research, handoffs, readiness, and evolving implementation context.
Runtime contracts that code depends on belong under packages.
This hub owns the public website design package, research inputs, and implementation sequence.

history.note: Folded temporary docs, research, prompts, and high-fidelity design artifacts into this active hub so root docs do not become stale parallel truth.
Recorded the data-first foundation wave before visual implementation: Keystatic shape, private intake APIs, normalized migrations, and Green Goods impact cache wiring.

Completed the public Schema Delta Pass in 996ce51: Astro/Keystatic schemas now cover HiFi home, garden, library, stories, chapters, guilds, resources, and book compatibility surfaces; Keystatic access control is documented as local file-backed authoring only.

Completed the foundation pass before page implementation: ported HiFi gp-tokens.css into packages/website with only the Astro public asset URL adapter, added a noindex /design-system token and component review surface, and introduced the new GpLayout, public shell components, and reusable Astro primitives under packages/website. GpLayout preserves the existing stylized background image by default while keeping the HiFi topographic texture as an opt-in section utility.
Addressed foundation review findings: made link-like primitives render non-clickable markup when no URL is supplied and added the skip-to-content shell accessibility path.

Restored Garden shell navigation and added a minimal public /garden route backed by the existing garden singleton with public-safe fallbacks, leaving the full HiFi Garden page for its focused implementation pass.

Completed Step 3.5 content grounding before Home implementation: added a source-backed content seed inventory, seeded public Keystatic content across the target collections, added an optional idempotent public runtime seed fixture/script for approved map nodes and one normalized chapter impact snapshot, and validated build plus plan and runtime contract checks.

Addressed the production review findings for Step 3.5: review-gated chapters are excluded from generated chapter pages, the chapter index, locations.json, and impact source exports; seeded stories now have draft status and are filtered from public story routes until steward approval; SEO canonical/noindex metadata is wired through active layouts; and unhealthy Germany/Uncommons proof links were removed from launch content.

Implemented the map polish and Live Onboarding Mode slice: admin-owned intake settings, live auto-approval with private audit rows, public-safe intakeMode map state, theme edge rendering, distinct chapter/steward/member node colors, and a responsive add-node flow.

Addressed the second production review findings: moved Live Onboarding settings into a replay-safe 003 migration, removed public refs to draft stories and noindex chapters, added a public content contract test for generated chapter/published story refs, and refreshed the Step 3.5 readiness notes.

Captured the Email Magic Link Node Updates pass as plan-only work: email-only ownership for submitted map nodes, hashed one-use edit tokens, pending update requests, neutral edit-link responses, steward approval before public changes, and no wallet/local-secret ownership in v1.

Resolved remaining map-plan gaps for the Email Magic Link Node Updates pass: required email on new submissions, exact public route shapes, 15-minute edit-link cooldown, token validation versus consumption semantics, editable public field list, and neutral responses for no-match and cooldown cases.

Added final hardening notes for the Email Magic Link Node Updates plan: neutral email-provider failure behavior, raw-token hygiene before analytics/navigation, and Directus permission boundaries for token/email/request metadata.

Added final implementation-readiness gaps to the map and email-update plan: migration ordering, role/type edit boundaries, stale approval protection, token/request retention, live map cache behavior, broader edit-link abuse limits, Directus review ergonomics, and accessible map decluttering.
